function_v0.doc: OpenZeppelin / `stellar-tokens` vault naming: underlying asset set at `initialize`.\nPanics if the vault was never initialized (same as [`Vault::query_asset`]).
function_v0.name: query_asset

function_v0.doc: Fungible share ledger lives on this contract (same pattern as `splyce-stellar-s-token` SToken).
function_v0.name: share_token

function_v0.doc:
Vault-as-matcher: invoke configured settlement engine `match_request` with `caller = this` (principal pulled from vault).

**Authorization:** `caller` must be the borrow request **creator**, a settlement engine **processor**, or the settlement **admin**.
The engine address is always read from vault config (not a parameter).

Soroban forbids re-entering this contract from within `match_request`, so post-match accounting
(`sync_after_principal_matched`) runs here after `match_request` returns instead of via an SE callback.
function_v0.name: execute_match

function_v0.doc:
Move **surplus** underlying (ledger balance minus [`Self::total_assets`] bookkeeping for the
payout token for the current status) to `recipient`. Only the settlement engine may call.

Use when users mistakenly transfer tokens into the vault, or other dust accumulates. Does
not change `TotalAssets` (LP claims are unchanged).
function_v0.name: sweep_surplus

function_v0.doc: Pro-rata conversion preview (current `TotalAssets` / share supply). **Not** claimable exit:\nuse `max_redeem` / `max_withdraw` to know whether `redeem` / `burn` will succeed\n(redemption is gated until Settled / Defaulted / Closed; see protocol docs).
function_v0.name: preview_redeem

function_v0.doc: Shares required to withdraw `assets` at current book (rounded up). **Not** claimable exit:\nuse `max_withdraw` / `max_redeem` for whether exit is allowed.
function_v0.name: preview_withdraw

function_v0.doc: Borrower cancel penalty: principal token already transferred into this contract; bumps\n`TotalAssets` without minting shares (pro-rata bonus to LPs).
function_v0.name: receive_cancel_penalty

function_v0.doc: After settlement_engine pulls `amount` of principal from this vault, sync book value and go Active.\nPrefer routing matches through [`Self::execute_match`]; this entrypoint remains for non-reentrant call paths.
function_v0.name: record_match_principal

fields.doc: Address that supplied principal at match (EOA, lending vault, or other contract).
fields.name: lender

udt_union_v0.doc: SEP-040-style asset id for oracle `lastprice` (Stellar smart-contract / SAC token).
udt_union_v0.name: OracleAsset

fields.doc: Loan-to-value at offer (basis points, e.g. 6000 = 60%). Used for buyout anchor when no oracle on loan.
fields.name: ltv_bps

udt_struct_v0.doc: Schedule step: at repayment, elapsed loan time selects the active tier via `from_seconds`.\n`rate_bps` is basis points of **principal** owed for that tier\xe2\x80\x94not APR/APY (no annualization).
udt_struct_v0.name: InterestRateTier

udt_struct_v0.doc: Packed oracle + liquidation price for `create_loan` (Soroban 10-arg limit).
udt_struct_v0.name: OracleLiquidation

cases.doc: `share_name` / `share_symbol` length or emptiness (same rules as factory / offer book).

cases.name: NoPendingUpgrade

cases.doc: Upgrade timelock has not yet expired.
cases.name: UpgradeTimelockActive

cases.doc: Empty or too-long `share_name` / `share_symbol` for `deploy_vault_for_request`.
cases.name: InvalidShareMetadata

udt_union_v0.doc:
Hook types for modular compliance system.

Each hook type represents a specific event or validation point
where compliance modules can be executed.
udt_union_v0.name: ComplianceHook

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully transferred from one wallet to\nanother. Modules registered for this hook can update their state\nbased on transfer events.
void_v0.name: Transferred

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully created/minted to a wallet.\nModules registered for this hook can update their state based on minting\nevents.
void_v0.name: Created

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully destroyed/burned from a wallet.\nModules registered for this hook can update their state based on burning\nevents.
void_v0.name: Destroyed

void_v0.doc:
Called during transfer validation to check if a transfer should be
allowed. Modules registered for this hook can implement transfer
restrictions. This is a READ-only operation and should not modify
state.
void_v0.name: CanTransfer

void_v0.doc:
Called during mint validation to check if a mint operation should be
allowed. Modules registered for this hook can implement transfer
restrictions. This is a READ-only operation and should not modify
state.
void_v0.name: CanCreate

cases.doc: Indicates a module is already registered for this hook.
cases.name: ModuleAlreadyRegistered

cases.doc: Indicates a module is not registered for this hook.
cases.name: ModuleNotRegistered

cases.doc: Indicates a module bound is exceeded.
cases.name: ModuleBoundExceeded

cases.doc: Indicates a token is not bound to this compliance contract.
cases.name: TokenNotBound

udt_union_v0.name: DataKey

udt_union_v0.doc:
Storage keys for the token binder system.

- Tokens are stored in buckets of 100 addresses each
- Each bucket is a `Vec<Address>` stored under its bucket index
- Total count is tracked separately
- When a token is unbound, the last token is moved to fill the gap
(swap-remove pattern)
udt_union_v0.name: TokenBinderStorageKey

tuple_v0.doc: Maps bucket index to a vector of token addresses (max 100 per bucket)

cases.doc: Indicates overflow when adding two values
cases.name: MathOverflow

cases.doc: Indicates access to uninitialized metadata
cases.name: UnsetMetadata

cases.doc: Indicates that the operation would have caused `total_supply` to exceed\nthe `cap`.
cases.name: ExceededCap

cases.doc: Indicates the supplied `cap` is not a valid cap value.
cases.name: InvalidCap

cases.doc: Indicates the Cap was not set.
cases.name: CapNotSet

cases.doc: Indicates the SAC address was not set.
cases.name: SACNotSet

cases.doc: Indicates a SAC address different than expected.
cases.name: SACAddressMismatch

cases.doc: Indicates a missing function parameter in the SAC contract context.
cases.name: SACMissingFnParam

cases.doc: Indicates an invalid function parameter in the SAC contract context.
cases.name: SACInvalidFnParam

cases.doc: The user is not allowed to perform this operation
cases.name: UserNotAllowed

cases.doc: The user is blocked and cannot perform this operation
cases.name: UserBlocked
